description_long: This object is a handheld two-way radio transceiver, commonly known as a walkie-talkie. It features a prominent black antenna at the top, a rectangular body with a textured grip, and a front-facing control panel with a numeric keypad and several colored buttons for operation. The front display is a small digital screen, likely LCD, showing channel and status information, flanked by green and red function buttons. The back side has a battery compartment with visible screws and a barcode sticker, while the sides include a large orange push-to-talk button and additional controls. The casing appears to be made of durable black plastic, designed for rugged use in urban or outdoor environments, and the overall build suggests portability and ease of use for communication.
description: A rugged black handheld walkie-talkie with a long antenna, digital display, keypad, and side control buttons. Designed for portable two-way communication in urban settings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the slim profile, antenna, and part of the front display and keypad.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the battery compartment, barcode sticker, and back screws.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the large orange push-to-talk button and side panel.
description_view_3: Front view featuring the digital display, full keypad, colored function buttons, and antenna.
